title: Week of April 6, 2026
summary: This week focused on major platform stability improvements that make OpsPal more reliable for daily use. The platform now automatically cleans up old files and fixes connection issues in the background, while new safeguards prevent common Salesforce setup errors that could block your workflows. These behind-the-scenes improvements mean fewer interruptions and smoother automation runs.

entries.headline: Platform automatically cleans up old files during updates
details: Removes outdated cache files older than 7 days
Clears stuck session files after 5 minutes
Warns when memory logs get too large

entries.headline: Platform removes broken plugin references automatically
details: Works across all operating systems including Windows, Linux, and Mac
Detects and removes any broken plugin connections
Prevents errors from outdated plugin configurations

entries.headline: New safeguards prevent common Salesforce configuration errors
details: Stops invisible field additions when Dynamic Forms is active
Automatically assigns new record types to prevent permission errors
Converts blocking routing errors to warnings to keep sessions running

entries.headline: Platform automatically fixes missing dependencies at startup
details: Installs required components automatically in the background
Shows clear error messages when manual fixes are needed
Prevents session failures from missing dependencies

entries.headline: Resolved false missing package errors at session start
